prompt: Tell me about a time you were confidently wrong about something and changed your mind. I want to hear what specifically caused you to update.
Walk me through your biggest accomplishment.
Tell me about a hire you made that didn't work out. What did you miss in the evaluation?
Describe a situation where you had to influence a decision without having positional authority.
Tell me about a time you pushed back on a direction, and later realized you were the one who was wrong.
Describe your leadership style.
Tell me about a technology choice you were sure about that underperformed in production.
Walk me through how you handled a disagreement with a peer you respected.
Tell me about a time you had to ship with incomplete information. What did you assume, and which of those assumptions turned out to be wrong?
Tell me about a time you removed a process or meeting and the team got faster.
What's something you believed about building software five years ago that you no longer believe?
Describe a time you gave feedback that landed badly. What did you learn about how you deliver feedback?
Tell me about a project where your initial estimate was dramatically wrong. What did you not account for?
Walk me through a time you had to lead a team through ambiguous direction from leadership.
Describe a moment when you had to reverse a decision you'd publicly committed to. How did you handle the reversal?
Tell me about your best technical decision of the last two years.
Tell me about a time you misread a room — thought you had buy-in and didn't. What did you miss?
Describe a time a direct report surprised you in a way that changed how you thought about them.
Walk me through a time you were the lone voice advocating for a position. Was your position the right one in hindsight?
Tell me about a time you had to operate entirely on your own judgment, with no one to check your work. What's the decision from that period you most second-guess now?
